generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving identifier references and type mismatches reported by the AGREE validator.
Prioritize fixing errors in expressions before attempting architectural changes.
Use only visible AADL features or helper variables declared within the current AGREE annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for scalar type declarations.
Ensure both sides of an equality (`=`) have the same type.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce unqualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Avoid adding architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract itself.
Do not place guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL scope rules for component types and implementations
